audit_status.source_base: Greek Hebrews text tradition; draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for mature foundation, severe warning, better things, Abraham promise, oath, anchor of soul.

verses.restored: Therefore, leaving the beginning word of Messiah, let us be carried toward maturity, not again laying foundation of turning from dead works and trust toward Divine,
teaching of washings, laying on of hands, resurrection of dead ones, and age-long judgment.
And this we will do, if the Divine permits.
For it is impossible for those once enlightened, tasting the heavenly gift, becoming partners of holy Spirit,
and tasting beautiful/good word of Divine and powers of coming age,
and falling away, to renew again into turning, since they are re-crucifying for themselves the Son of the Divine and exposing him to public shame.
For earth drinking rain often coming upon it and bringing forth useful plant for those through whom it is cultivated shares blessing from the Divine.
But bearing thorns and thistles is disapproved and near curse, whose end is burning.
But concerning you, beloved, we have been persuaded of better things, things holding to salvation/healing, even if we speak this way.
For the Divine is not unjust to forget your work and the love you showed toward his name, having served the holy ones and still serving.
But we desire each of you to show the same eagerness toward full assurance of hope until completion,
so that you may not become sluggish, but imitators of those through trust and patience inheriting the promises.
For when the Divine promised Abraham, since he had no one greater by whom to swear, he swore by himself,
saying: Surely blessing I will bless you, and multiplying I will multiply you.
And so, having patiently endured, he obtained the promise.
For humans swear by the greater, and for them oath is end of every dispute as confirmation.
In this, the Divine, desiring more abundantly to show to heirs of promise the unchangeable nature of his purpose, mediated with oath,
so that through two unchangeable things, in which it is impossible for Divine to lie, we who fled for refuge might have strong encouragement to seize the hope set before us.
We have this as anchor of soul/life, secure and firm and entering inside the veil,
where Jesus entered as forerunner on our behalf, having become high priest into the age according to order of Melchizedek.

translation_notes: Hebrews 6 warning is severe but immediately followed by confidence in better things and Divine remembrance of love-service.
Anchor imagery is central: hope enters inside the veil through Jesus as forerunner.
Abraham promise/oath continuity should not be read as replacement of Israel but as trust in Divine promise.
safety_note: Do not use Hebrews 6 to terrorize tender consciences. The text warns against catastrophic abandonment while assuring the beloved community that their love-service is remembered by a just Divine.
